articles.title: Scientists say this type of olive oil could boost brain power
articles.description: Extra virgin olive oil might help protect your brain by working through your gut. A two-year study found that people who consumed it had better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria than those using refined olive oil. Researchers even identified specific microbes linked to these benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality olive oil could be a simple way to support brain health as you age.

articles.title: Pinpointing barriers to timely head and neck cancer treatment in rural areas
articles.description: A new study led by researchers at Dartmouth Cancer Center pinpoints why many patients in rural areas experience delays in receiving critical follow-up treatment for head and neck cancer, and what can be done to address those gaps. Published in JAMA Otolaryngology–Head & Neck Surgery, the study examines factors that influence whether patients begin postoperative radiotherapy within the recommended six-week window after surgery, the timeframe known to improve survival for patients with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

articles.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed in response to U.S. Navy blockade
articles.description: In a major reversal, Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, less than 24 hours after it announced the critical waterway was “completely open.” It comes as Iran allegedly opened fire on two ships attempting to pass through the strait. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said the closure is a response to the continuing U.S. Navy blockade on Iranian ports. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest.

articles.title: Experimental treatment for pancreatic cancer could be breakthrough for patients
articles.description: New data finds an experimental cancer treatment is showing major promise. articles.title: The GCC has unity, it now needs joint defence and development
articles.description: Beyond supporting peace, GCC states need to work towards joint defence, energy security and economic development.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CEO flags off marathon for awareness
articles.description: About 3,000 people took part in the event aimed at creating awareness to ensure a 100% polling percentage in the April 23 Assembly election in Tamil Nadu

articles.title: Total value of election-related seizures in Villupuram district reach ₹89.48 lakh
articles.description: While conducting vehicle checks, the teams had been seizing cash found carried without valid documents at various places in seven Assembly constituencies across Villupuram

articles.title: One arrested for hurling a glass bottle at the convoy of TVK candidate in Saidapet
articles.description: Sampath, a car mechanic, hurled an empty liquor bottle at TVK members when the party’s Saidapet candidate Arul Prakasam was canvassing votes in the locality

articles.title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Improvement of tourism facilities a key demand in Valparai constituency
articles.description: Valparai (Reserve) Assembly constituency has two segments, one in the plains and the other is the hills, where the prime occupation is tea cultivation.

topStories.title: This tariff-refund portal is about to be America's hottest website
topStories.description: Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.

topStories.title: Days after Oval Office burger order, Trump says move was ‘embarassing’: ‘We do these things in politics’
topStories.description: The US President personally received McDonald's cheeseburgers, which were delivered to the Oval Office by DoorDash driver Sharon Simmons, a Republican supporter.

topStories.title: US Deploys Warships, Attack Helicopters As Tensions Rise With Iran
topStories.description: US Navy ramps up presence in the Strait of Hormuz as CENTCOM enforces a total maritime blockade. USS Rushmore and warships halt trade amid Trump's vow to pressure Iran by April 22.

topStories.title: Supreme Court weighs phone searches to find criminals amid complaints of 'digital dragnets'
topStories.description: Does the 4th Amendment's ban on unreasonable searches extend to your smartphone and its tracking data?

topStories.title: Ordered free, still locked up: Judges fume as Trump administration holds ICE detainees
topStories.description: A legal maneuver once reserved for death row inmates and suspected terrorists has become the only recourse for immigrant detainees, who have flooded federal courts in California with thousands of petitions for freedom.

topStories.title: A renewed threat to JPL as the Trump administration tries again to cut NASA
topStories.description: The cuts, proposed in the Trump administration's 2027 budget request to Congress, would pose further challenges to the venerable La Cañada Flintridge facility and its space missions.

topStories.title: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
topStories.description: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

topStories.title: ‘Your resume is worth nothing in 2026’: Viral post exposes how random tech hiring has become
topStories.description: A viral social media post highlights the brutal hiring reality at a US startup, where a founder received 6,840 applications for three positions. This has ignited a debate on the significance of elite degrees and the role of luck in job hunting.

topStories.title: 'Ready to give up for a sister': Congress’ Manickam Tagore offers to vacate seat if women's quota applied to current constituencies
topStories.description: Following the defeat of the Women's Reservation Bill in Lok Sabha, Congress MP Manickam Tagore challenged PM Modi and Amit Shah to give up their seats for women candidates if 33% reservation is implemented across existing constituencies.

topStories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 51 of the US-Iran conflict?
topStories.description: Tehran will keep the strategic Strait of Hormuz closed until Washington ends the blockade of Iranian ports.

Luxury Brands Bet on the Middle East. War Has Damaged Their Plans.
dailyNews.description: High-end brands such as Louis Vuitton and Hermès are looking to other regions as sales in Persian Gulf nations plummet.

dailyNews.title: Are Democrats Becoming a Party of Tax Cuts?
dailyNews.description: As Democrats try to find a way back to power in Washington, some see tax cuts as a quick and easy way to address affordability. The wonks are freaking out.

dailyNews.title: In Qatar, Trapped Between the U.S. and Iran, War Forced a Reckoning
dailyNews.description: The gas-rich Gulf nation is in a state of “strategic shock” after the war dealt a serious blow to its economy, sending ripples around the world.

dailyNews.title: Syrian Billionaires Needed a Favor in Washington. They Invoked the Trump Name.
dailyNews.description: The attempt by the Khayyats to influence foreign policy while discussions are underway about potential Trump family deals is an increasingly common feature of the president’s second term.

dailyNews.title: We analyzed Trump’s public remarks. His White House ballroom is a top priority.
dailyNews.description: The president has publicly highlighted his White House ballroom project on roughly a third of the days this year, a Washington Post analysis found.

dailyNews.title: In Angola, love for an American pope but not for an American president
dailyNews.description: In Luanda, Catholics expressed love and admiration for Pope Leo XIV, the first American pope, but not so much for President Trump because of the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: No more ‘corridors of peril’: Supreme Court rolls out strict highway safety norms, bans illegal parking, encroachments
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court has mandated nationwide road safety improvements, banning heavy vehicle parking on national highways and restricting new roadside structures.  Authorities must ensure compliance within 60 days, with district magistrates overseeing inspections. Task forces will be formed to address accident-prone 'blackspots' and enhance surveillance, aiming to prevent avoidable fatalities.

dailyNews.title: DANA PERINO: 'Purple State' reveals life's biggest choices are personal, not political
dailyNews.description: Dana Perino bridges her mentoring book 'Everything Will Be Okay' with her new novel "Purple State," following three women through quarter-life crises.

dailyNews.title: School choice is booming in Iowa. Are students better off?
dailyNews.description: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.

dailyNews.title: Police investigating potential shooting near University of Iowa, campus says
dailyNews.description: "First responders on scene. Confirmed victims," the university said in an alert.

dailyNews.title: Titanic survivor's life jacket sells for over $900K at auction, far exceeding price expectations
dailyNews.description: A life jacket worn by Titanic survivor Laura Mabel Francatelli sold for roughly $906,000 at auction, far exceeding its pre-sale estimate of $475,000.

dailyNews.title: Buoyed by Brunson, Knicks seal 'hard-fought win'
dailyNews.description: Jalen Brunson crossed the 20-point mark for the 38th time in a Knicks uniform, finishing with 28 on Saturday at Madison Square Garden, and New York posted a 113-102 victory over the Atlanta Hawks in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round series.

dailyNews.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey and Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky spoke at a gathering of party insiders in Detroit, fueling presidential speculation.

dailyNews.title: With Trump Novices, Can the U.S. Win the ‘Art Olympics’?
dailyNews.description: After the State Department overhauled the process for choosing an artist for the Venice Biennale, it gave control to a woman who previously owned a pet food store.

dailyNews.title: They’re Young. They’re Conservative. And They’re Split Over the War.
dailyNews.description: On college campuses from the Northeast to the Southwest, the conflict in Iran is testing the fealty of young Republicans during the second Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: How the dispute between Trump and Pope Leo escalated
dailyNews.description: President Trump has lobbed insults at Pope Leo XIV in response to his criticisms of the war in Iran, marking an unusually pronounced rupture between the leaders of the world's most powerful country and the world's largest Christian denomination.

dailyNews.title: Pope Leo says he is not trying to debate Trump, but preach peace in Africa
dailyNews.description: Pope Leo pushed back on claims that he is feuding with President Trump. Chris Livesay has more details.

dailyNews.title: Americans see some relief at the pump
dailyNews.description: President Trump accused Iran of blackmailing the U.S. on Saturday, but there is some relief for Americans. Gas prices fell, averaging just over $4 a gallon nationwide. Olivia Rinaldi reports from the White House on the war's ripple effects.

dailyNews.title: 4/18: CBS Weekend News
dailyNews.description: CBS News on the Strait of Hormuz; Trump seems optimistic for a diplomatic end to the war.

dailyNews.description: Joe Rogan says he sent Trump information on ibogaine

dailyNews.title: Trump jokes about asking for ibogaine at executive order signing
dailyNews.description: President Trump jokes about asking for ibogaine at an executive order signing directing the FDA to review psychedelic drugs designated as breakthrough therapy.

dailyNews.title: White House holds press briefing
dailyNews.description: Watch live coverage as White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt holds a press briefing.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he had 'no idea' Anthropic's Amodei met with White House about Mythos
dailyNews.description: The meeting comes less than two months after President Donald Trump blacklisted Anthropic's Claude.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.
